title: Canonical queue state lags append-only team evidence
observed_claims: The canonical ACTIVE_QUEUE snapshot records frontier work only through R-004.
Later repository commits preserve completed R-008 work and newer C-001 progress outside the canonical snapshot.
M-002 explicitly preserves the distinction between committed implementation and externally verified deployment.
inferred_claims: A reader using only ACTIVE_QUEUE.json can undercount completed work and select already-addressed research gaps.
Append-only queue updates currently function as the fresher operational ledger, but no repository artifact defines a deterministic reconciliation rule.
proposed_claims: Add a reconciliation artifact that computes effective queue state from the canonical snapshot plus ordered append-only updates.
Retain the canonical snapshot as historical input rather than rewriting or deleting it during reconciliation.
unresolved_claims: Which artifact is authoritative when the canonical queue and append-only updates disagree?
What ordering and conflict-resolution rule should apply when two teams update the same queue item concurrently?

contradicts.left: ACTIVE_QUEUE.json appears complete as a canonical operational queue
contradicts.right: Newer append-only updates contain material state absent from that snapshot
contradicts.resolution: Treat this as temporal lag, not evidence that either historical artifact is false. Preserve both and require reconciliation before work selection.

verification: Fetched the canonical queue blob, specialist registry, trigger ledger, continuity index, recent commit list, and M-002 commit during this run.
falsification_or_review_route: Re-read ACTIVE_QUEUE.json at blob abb807e1a25d399f301238f6b8cc26d6be10385f and inspect the cited later commits. Falsify the lag claim if the snapshot already contains equivalent R-008, newer C-001, and M-002 state, or if the cited commits do not contain those updates.
next_action: Implement and test a deterministic effective-queue reconciler that reports conflicts without overwriting source artifacts.
